subject: Re: deselected micrographs
body:
Hi Yong-Hui,

deleting the XML metadata files for the faulty micrographs from the project 
folder and restarting Warp is still the preferred solution. If they are too 
scattered and can't be selected easily, inside Warp in the navigation bar 
(Fourier or Realspace tabs) you can click the magnifying glass icon and 
uncheck all the filter checkboxes except the grey one. This will leave you 
with only the faulty ones in the navigation bar. Then you can use the arrow 
keys on the keyboard and the Ctrl+F shortcut to quickly go through them and 
set their status.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, June 4, 2019 at 12:24:32 PM UTC+2, Yong-Hui Sung wrote:
>
> Hi, 
>
> I do get the same issue a bunch of micrographs just get randomly 
> deselected in my case its 600 out 6000. If I manually select them they seem 
> to work with the current settings.  However, it is quite some effort to get 
> them back by clicking in the gui or finding the respective XML files. 
>
> Is there a faster way? 
>
> best, 
>
> YHS
>
> On Wednesday, March 6, 2019 at 4:44:35 PM UTC+1, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>
>> Hi Gabor,
>>
>> the easiest would be to delete the corresponding .xml files and restart 
>> Warp. Items become deselected if something goes wrong during processing. In 
>> most cases the CTF is unfittable with the current parameters, or you don't 
>> have write permissions to save the results. It would be important to figure 
>> out what went wrong, otherwise the items will turn grey again.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Monday, March 4, 2019 at 1:28:22 PM UTC+1, Gabor Papai wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>
>>> I had a strange behavior of Warp this weekend: after processing around 
>>> 3500 tiffs all the new entries got deselected and they were not processed. 
>>> I don't know why this happened. 
>>> I can re-select the tiffs manually, but I don't know how to re-select 
>>> them all, not one-by-one.
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Gabor
>>>
>>
